title: Mothers
questions: What images spring to mind when you hear the word 'mother'?
Can you describe your mother in one word? Why that word?
Have you ever thought that mothers might have ESP?
Do you always buy your mother a present on Mother's Day?
What are the differences between a mother and a mother-in-law?
In what ways do you take after your mother?
What is your mother's biggest fault?
Is your mother more like a mother or a best friend?
What do/did your mother and you argue about?
What do you think of Mother's Day? Is it necessary?
What are the biggest differences between mothers and fathers?
Why and how did your father fall in love with your mother?
Are you happy with the way your mother raised you?
Should mothers stay at home and look after the kids?
What's the best piece of advice your mother has given you?
Do you think mothers are the same all around the world?
How are mothers and grandmothers different?
What do you think is the hardest thing about being a mother?
What's the biggest lie you've ever told your mother?
What do you talk to your mother about on the phone?
